steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says red causes "caution and hesitation" in people who encounter it. The blank asks what effect Manchester United’s red home uniforms may have had. The correct choice must describe a consequence of that caution-and-hesitation response in the context of athletic competition — and the agent experiencing it must be the opposing players, not Manchester United.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — increased Manchester United players' sense of excitement: The passage says red triggers caution in those who see it, not excitement in those who wear it. Wrong agent, wrong effect. ✗
  • Choice B — negatively affected the performance of players on opposing teams: Opposing players see the red uniforms, respond with caution and hesitation, and their on-field performance suffers — exactly the causal chain the passage sets up. ✓
  • Choice C — led fans to perceive the team's opponents as more dominant than they actually were: Fan perception is never discussed; the passage is about players responding to red. ✗
  • Choice D — caused Manchester United's players to play less skillfully: Manchester United wears the red uniforms; they would not be inhibited by their own color. Wrong agent. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
